shortDescription.text: Identifies skill descriptions or documentation that are confirmed to be benign and serve a legitimate purpose.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags content that, despite initial scrutiny for malicious patterns, is verified to be non-malicious. This includes documentation, architectural descriptions, or configuration files that contain references to internal commands or system functionalities which are confirmed by external threat intelligence to be part of legitimate software operation, without exhibiting any exploit primitives, malicious infrastructure, or unsafe practices within the analyzed content itself.

message.text: The analysis of the SKILL.md content, which describes the 'LomoMarketplace' project and its internal commands (/dev-helper:init, /update-arch, /track-module), indicates no confirmed malicious intent. External threat intelligence from Claude Code documentation (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/discover-plugins) strongly CONFIRMS the legitimate use of such administrative commands within a plugin marketplace environment. No executable code, external network calls, file system manipulation, credential handling, or obfuscation was found within the Markdown content. The behavior described is consistent with legitimate skill management within the 'Claude Code Plugin Marketplace' ecosystem. No exploit primitives or malicious infrastructure were identified. The initial analysis of 'LIKELY_BENIGN' is fully confirmed.
